### What I changed

- **Updated frontend Stripe URL** in `scripts.js`:

```173:175:/Users/marko/Documents/Code/ubi-workshop/scripts.js
// Replace with production Stripe Checkout link
const checkoutUrl = 'https://buy.stripe.com/4gM28qbBJ6522Hk1mmafS07';
```

- **Updated email Stripe URL** in `api/reserve.js`:

```106:110:/Users/marko/Documents/Code/ubi-workshop/api/reserve.js
<p style="margin: 0 0 12px;">Thanks for reserving for our ${cohortDateFormatted} cohort (SF). If you didn't finish checkout, use this link:</p>
<p style="margin: 0 16px 16px;"><a href="https://buy.stripe.com/4gM28qbBJ6522Hk1mmafS07" style="color:#6B46C1;">Complete checkout</a></p>
```

### Deployment to Vercel

- **Committed and pushed** the change to `origin/master` (`Update Stripe checkout link`), which should trigger your existing Vercel Git integration to build and deploy `ubi-workshop.vercel.app`.  
- Once Vercel finishes, the live site and confirmation emails will both point to `https://buy.stripe.com/4gM28qbBJ6522Hk1mmafS07`.

### Scalability / maintainability note

This change keeps the Stripe URL duplicated in frontend and backend; for long-term maintainability, centralizing it (e.g. a shared config file or environment variable like `STRIPE_CHECKOUT_URL`) would remove the risk of them drifting. As you evolve pricing or checkout flows, I’d suggest extracting that URL into a single config source and wiring both the client and API to read from it, so future updates are one-line changes with lower chance of mistakes.

---

### What’s going on

- `https://ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app` is a **preview deployment** that has the new code.
- `https://workshop.ubi.studio` is wired to the **project’s production deployment**, which is still an **older build**.
- Our recent push to `master` created (or updated) a **preview**; it did **not** become the production deployment that the custom domain uses.

This almost always means either:
- The **Production Branch** in Vercel is not `master` (e.g. set to `main`), so pushes to `master` only create previews, or  
- The correct deployment hasn’t been **promoted to production** yet.

### How to fix it in Vercel

Do this in the Vercel dashboard for the `ubi-course` (or equivalent) project:

1. **Check / fix Production Branch**
   - Go to **Settings → Git**.
   - Under **Production Branch**, set it to `master` (or to whatever branch we’re actually pushing to).
   - Save.

2. **Promote the good deployment to production**
   - Go to the project’s **Deployments** tab.
   - Find the deployment whose URL is `ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app`.
   - Click **“Promote to Production”** (or “Set as Production”) so it becomes the production deployment.

3. **Verify domain mapping**
   - In **Settings → Domains**, confirm `workshop.ubi.studio` is attached to **this project** and not to some old project.
   - If it appears on another project, remove it there and add it here.

After those steps, `https://workshop.ubi.studio` should serve the same content as `https://ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app` once Vercel finishes the promotion and caches clear.

### Quick reality check

I can’t click buttons in your Vercel dashboard or use your credentials, so I can’t literally flip the switches myself. I can, however, walk you through the minimal exact steps to get `workshop.ubi.studio` serving the new build that’s already live at `ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app`.

### Do this in Vercel (precise steps)

1. **Open the correct project**
   - In Vercel, open the project whose deployments list includes `https://ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app`.

2. **Promote that deployment to Production**
   - Go to the project’s **Deployments** tab.
   - Locate the deployment with URL `ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app`.
   - Click the `...` menu on that deployment and select **“Promote to Production”** (or similarly named action).

3. **Ensure the Production Branch is right**
   - Go to **Settings → Git** for this project.
   - Under **Production Branch**, set it to `master` (the branch we just pushed to) and save.

4. **Confirm the domain is attached to this project**
   - Go to **Settings → Domains**.
   - Make sure `workshop.ubi.studio` is listed here on this project (not on some older one).
   - If it appears on another project, remove it there and add it here.

Once step 2 completes and any caches settle, `https://workshop.ubi.studio` should show exactly what you see on `https://ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app`. If you do these and it’s still wrong, tell me what you see on the Deployments and Domains screens and I’ll diagnose the next layer.

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

Totally get that you just want this live, but I need to be blunt: **I cannot press buttons inside your Vercel account from here**. I’ve already pushed the repo; the only thing left is to tell Vercel “this is production now”, and that has to be done in their UI.

Here’s the shortest possible path to get `workshop.ubi.studio` serving the build that’s already at `ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app`:

### 1. Promote the correct deployment

1. In Vercel, open the project that has the URL `https://ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app` in its **Deployments** list.
2. Go to the **Deployments** tab.
3. Find the row with that exact URL.
4. Click the `…` menu on the right and choose **“Promote to Production”**.

That alone will usually flip production to the right version.

### 2. Make sure future pushes auto-deploy

1. In the same project, go to **Settings → Git**.
2. Under **Production Branch**, set it to `master` (the branch we’re pushing to).
3. Save.

### 3. Confirm the domain is attached to this project

1. Go to **Settings → Domains**.
2. Ensure `workshop.ubi.studio` is listed there on **this** project.
   - If it shows up on some older project, remove it there and add it here instead.

After step 1, give it a minute and then hit `https://workshop.ubi.studio?ts=<random>` in your browser to bypass cache. At that point it should be serving exactly the same content as the `ubi-course-49m0ul6an-0xmccs-projects.vercel.app` deployment.
